sanskrit: नियतं कुरु कर्म त्वं कर्म ज्यायो ह्यकर्मणः। शरीरयात्रापि च ते न प्रसिद्ध्येदकर्मणः॥
transliteration: niyataṃ kuru karma tvaṃ karma jyāyo hy akarmaṇaḥ | śarīra-yātrāpi ca te na prasiddhyed akarmaṇaḥ ||
literal_meaning: Perform the prescribed action; action is better than non-action; even bodily maintenance cannot be accomplished without action.
contextual_meaning: Krishna reminds Arjuna that healthy functioning requires engagement with life’s tasks; avoidance is not liberation but stagnation.
psychological_state: Approach-avoidance conflict: the ego wants to opt out of discomfort yet fears the consequences of inaction.
trigger_scenario: A professional contemplates quitting work to 'find themselves' but secretly expects parents/partner to pay the bills.
anchor_text: Engage fully with life; even survival depends on purposeful action.
